prose:
Method provenance.
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, f0) and dual descriptor for acrolein; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f+ = 0.64232102 (atom 4, Cβ of the carbonyl-conjugated C=C bond). Units are electrons (dimensionless indices; f+ sums to 1 by construction).
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (RI) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the fixed user-supplied geometry /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz; no additional optimization was performed (notation: fukui//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1) for the N state; cation doublet (+1,2) and anion doublet (−1,2) for the N±1 states used in the finite-difference scheme.
Software/backend: PySCF via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (cli invocation reproduced below).
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged (integrity.status = "ok"). The integrity checker issued three warnings, echoed verbatim below. Sum of Fukui indices f+ and f− each equals 1.0000, consistent with charge conservation. No other warnings reported.

CLI invocation (from result JSON):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpifkfo_fw/live.json

Live .out log: the run streamed to a time-stamped file in the caller’s working directory (announced when launched); retain for full SCF and Mulliken-charge details.
